entities.name: Claude Agent SDK (TypeScript)
entities.description: Anthropic's official runtime that powers Claude Code, packaged as a library. Supports subagents, tools, hooks, MCP servers, sessions. npm: @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk

entities.name: LangGraph.js
entities.description: Graph-based agent orchestration framework for TypeScript. 42k+ weekly npm downloads. Supports StateGraph, conditional edges, checkpointing, multi-agent supervisor/swarm patterns. npm: @langchain/langgraph

entities.name: LangChain.js
entities.description: Agent engineering platform for TypeScript. createAgent() builds graph-based agent runtime using LangGraph underneath. npm: langchain

entities.name: LangFuse
entities.description: Open-source LLM observability platform. TypeScript SDK built on OpenTelemetry. Supports traces, spans, generations with parent-child nesting. npm: @langfuse/tracing, @langfuse/otel

entities.name: MCP TypeScript SDK
entities.description: Official Model Context Protocol SDK. Tools, resources, prompts. Stdio and Streamable HTTP transports. Runs on Node.js, Bun, Deno. npm: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk

entities.name: Mastra
entities.description: TypeScript-native AI agent framework from Gatsby team. 22k+ GitHub stars, 300k+ weekly npm downloads. Agents, workflows, memory, evals, observability in single framework. npm: @mastra/core

entities.name: OpenAI Agents SDK (TypeScript)
entities.description: OpenAI's production successor to Swarm. Handoffs, guardrails, tracing. Provider-agnostic. npm: @openai/agents-sdk

entities.name: CrewAI
entities.description: Python-only role-based multi-agent framework. YAML config for agent definitions (role, goal, backstory). Python only -- no TypeScript support.

entities.name: Orchestrator-Worker Pattern
entities.description: Central LLM dynamically breaks down tasks, delegates to worker LLMs, synthesizes results. Anthropic's recommended pattern for complex multi-agent systems. Subtasks not pre-defined but determined by orchestrator.

entities.name: Supervisor Pattern
entities.description: Centralized coordinator agent receives requests and routes to specialist workers. Examines state to decide which worker handles each step. LangGraph's primary multi-agent pattern.

entities.name: Swarm / Handoff Pattern
entities.description: Agents autonomously decide when to hand off to each other without central coordinator. OpenAI Agents SDK uses this. @langchain/langgraph-swarm package in LangGraph.

entities.name: ReAct Agent Loop
entities.description: Reasoning + Acting pattern. Input -> model decision -> tool execution -> observation -> model reasoning -> output. Core loop used by LangChain createAgent(), Claude Agent SDK, and most agent frameworks.

entities.name: Anthropic Building Effective Agents Guide
entities.description: Official Anthropic guide. Key thesis: start simple, avoid frameworks early, use LLM APIs directly. Distinguishes workflows (predefined paths) from agents (dynamic control). Five workflow patterns: chaining, routing, parallelization, orchestrator-worker, evaluator-optimizer.

entities.name: Anthropic Multi-Agent Research System
entities.description: Production system using orchestrator-worker. Lead agent spawns 3-5 subagents in parallel. 15x more tokens than single-agent but 90.2% outperformance. Context windows managed via checkpointing. CitationAgent post-processes for attribution.

entities.name: LangfuseSpanProcessor
entities.description: OpenTelemetry SpanProcessor that exports spans to LangFuse. Key integration point. Supports immediate export mode for serverless.

entities.name: LangFuse CallbackHandler
entities.description: LangChain integration component. Automatically captures LLM calls, tools, retrievers. Works with LangGraph. Passes userId, sessionId, tags via metadata.

entities.name: MCP Tool Server
entities.description: Server exposing tools/resources/prompts via MCP protocol. Can be shared across multiple agents. Supports stdio (local) and Streamable HTTP (remote) transports.

entities.name: langchain-mcp-adapters
entities.description: Converts MCP tools into LangChain-compatible tools. Agent sees MCP servers like any other tool but calls go through MCP protocol.

entities.name: Shared Scratchpad Pattern
entities.description: In-memory or SQLite-backed shared state for single-session agent coordination. Low complexity. Agents write results and read siblings' results through a common store.

entities.name: Vector Similarity Memory
entities.description: Cosine similarity search over stored task results. Threshold 0.85+ for reuse. Dominates production multi-agent memory implementations. ChromaDB, Pinecone, Qdrant.

entities.name: SQLite Agent Memory
entities.description: Zero-config structured relational store. Perfect for single-process agents. MCP server runs locally. Queryable, durable. SQLite in-memory mode for ephemeral state.

entities.name: Microservices Consolidation Trend
entities.description: 42% of orgs consolidated microservices back to larger units (CNCF 2025). Service mesh adoption dropped from 18% to 8%. Primary drivers: debugging complexity, operational overhead, network latency.

entities.name: Monolith vs Microservice Performance Data
entities.description: Function call: 0.001ms vs HTTP: 1-5ms (1000-5000x slower). p99: 50ms vs 120ms (+140%). Memory: 512MB vs 2GB (+300%). Serialization per call: 0.85ms. 6-service chain: 5.1ms serialization overhead alone.

entities.name: YAML Agent Configuration Pattern
entities.description: Externalize agent role, goal, backstory, tools, model in YAML. CrewAI pioneered. Variables like {topic} replaced at runtime. Method names must match YAML keys. Decouples config from code.

entities.name: OpenClaw
entities.description: YAML-first multi-agent configuration. Lower entry barrier than Python class definitions. Enables non-technical stakeholders to participate in agent design.

entities.name: Claude SDK AgentDefinition
entities.description: Programmatic subagent definition. Fields: description (required), prompt (required), tools (optional), model (optional: sonnet/opus/haiku/inherit), skills, memory, mcpServers. Subagents cannot spawn their own subagents.

entities.name: OpenTelemetry Integration Pattern
entities.description: LangFuse SDK built on OTel. NodeSDK + LangfuseSpanProcessor. Context propagation for automatic parent-child nesting. propagateAttributes() for userId/sessionId across spans.

entities.name: Workstacean EventBus
entities.description: Existing typed EventBus with publish/subscribe, topic patterns, plugin name tracking. BusMessage with correlationId, parentId, source, reply routing.

entities.name: Workstacean Plugin Interface
entities.description: Existing Plugin interface: name, description, capabilities[], install(bus), uninstall(). Already used by PlannerPluginL0, ActionDispatcherPlugin, GoalEvaluatorPlugin, CeremonyPlugin, SkillBrokerPlugin.

entities.name: Workstacean World State / GOAP
entities.description: Existing PlannerState with cloneState, applyUpdate, stateKey, statesEqual. A* planner, HTN decomposer, action registry. L0/L1/L2 planning hierarchy.

entities.name: Workstacean LangfuseLogger
entities.description: Existing basic LangfuseLogger using HTTP ingestion API directly (no SDK). Logs goal violations. Has buffer+flush pattern. Could be upgraded to OTel-based SDK.

entities.name: Context Isolation Pattern
entities.description: Each subagent runs in fresh conversation. Intermediate tool calls stay inside subagent. Only final message returns to parent. Prevents context window bloat. Claude Agent SDK default behavior.

entities.name: Hierarchical Team Sizing
entities.description: Anthropic's production-tested sweet spot: 2-5 teammates with 5-6 tasks per teammate. Teams >5 specialists hit coordination overhead that cancels parallelism benefit. 2 delegation levels outperform flat by 28%; 3rd level adds only 7% with 40% more latency.

entities.name: Vercel AI SDK
entities.description: Lightweight LLM interaction layer used by Mastra. Handles model-agnostic streaming and tool calling. Alternative to direct OpenAI/Anthropic SDK usage.
